results.title: The 2-D shapes game
results.creator_credit: Stephen Bosustow and Stephen Bosustow Productions
results.brief_description: A magic drawing board introduces children to the 5 basic 2-dimensional shapes (square, circle, triangle, rectangle and ellipse). The film establishes the characteristics of regular plane shapes and helps children to recognize and name them. Suitable for primary grades.
results.description: A magic drawing board introduces children to the 5 basic 2-dimensional shapes (square, circle, triangle, rectangle and ellipse). The film establishes the characteristics of regular plane shapes and helps children to recognize and name them. Suitable for primary grades.

results.title: Prime time
results.brief_description: Shows how to distinguish between prime and composite numbers by presenting a story in which a specialist in space communications helps maintain contact with intelligent life on a newly discovered planet.

results.title: The Physicists: playing dice with the universe
results.creator_credit: Roger Hogen, D Leiterman, Philip S. Hobel and Document Associates
results.brief_description: Describes the work of physicists as they experiment with ideas which few can comprehend. Tells how some are mapping the tiniest structures of matter, others are listening to the universe, hoping to discover intelligent life, and others are looking for black holes.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Roger Hogen, D Leiterman, Philip S. Hobel and Document Associates

results.title: Measure metric. Vol. 3. Doc Cranshaw and the kid: the winner
results.brief_description:
The grandson of a medicine man wins a contest by mastering metric volume measurement. Demonstrates the use of formula, the relationship and the principle of water displacement to determine the volume of non-rectangular objects.
Suitable for middle and upper primary level.

results.title: Measure metric. Vol. 2. Doc Cranshaw and the kid: the contest
results.brief_description:
Demonstrates metric measurement of volume, using litres, millilitres, cubic centimetres and cubic decimetres. Includes a variety of processes for determining the volume of a rectangular solid.
Suitable for middle and upper primary level.

results.title: Measure metric. Vol. 1. Doc Cranshaw and the kid: the medicine show
results.brief_description:
A medicine man and his grandson learn about metric measurement from residents of a Utah frontier town. Demonstrates the litre and the millilitre and methods for determining the volume of liquid and granular substances.
Suitable for middle and upper primary level.

results.title: Area III: Barbara finds the formula
results.brief_description: A girl explores an imaginary place called Arealand where she learns the formula for the area of a rectangle: A = L x W, and meets the hectare and the square kilometre. No other credits available. Suitable for Mid-Upper Primary students.

results.title: Area II: Barbara searches the squares
results.brief_description: A girl explores an imaginary place called Arealand where she learns about the use of squares in metric measurment. Demonstrates how to determine area either by counting squares of by multiplying columns by rows. No other credits availabe. Suitable for Mid-Upper Primary students.

results.title: Measure metric: length 2: a thousand clicks
results.brief_description: Demonstrates how to measure circular objects and record the answers using decimal notation, how to measure lengths that are more than one metre, and how to use the trundle wheel to measure a kilometre. Suitable for middle and upper primary levels.

results.title: Measure metric: length 1: who measures?
results.brief_description: Two teenage boys explore metric measurement in downtown Toronto. Demonstrates proper length-measuring technqiues, using the appropriate measurement instrument, and learning the linea equivalents of 1m, 10cm, 1000cm. Suitable for middle and upper primary levels.
